creator: yokota@zodiac.mech.utsunomiya-u.ac.jp
text:
>>Number:         6650
>>Category:       kern
>>Synopsis:       Replugging PS/2 keyboard causes a panic
>>Confidential:   no
>>Severity:       serious
>>Priority:       medium
>>Responsible:    freebsd-bugs
>>State:          open
>>Quarter:
>>Keywords:
>>Date-Required:
>>Class:          sw-bug
>>Submitter-Id:   current-users
>>Arrival-Date:   Fri May 15 23:00:01 PDT 1998
>>Last-Modified:
>>Originator:     Heikki Suonsivu
>>Organization:
>Clinet, Espoo, Finland
>>Release:        FreeBSD 2.2.6-STABLE i386
>>Environment:
>
>Any FreeBSD 2.2-STABLE we have with PS/2 keyboard (ATX or proprietary
>motherboards).  Kernels vary but about display/keyboard related things we
>do use pcvt, if that matters. 
>
>>Description:
>
>When unplugging and replugging the keyboard, the system immediately
>panics.  This is highly repeatable here.  This happened only with PS/2 type
>keyboards before, but now we are seeing it in other types of hardware as
>well, so it might be that the rot is spreading.
[...]

Looking at your gdb output, I found that somehow the variable `kbdc'
is set to NULL and read_kbd_data_no_wait() is called with this NULL
pointer, leading to the system crash.

`kbdc' is set to NULL only if either the keyboard or the keyboard
controller cannot be initialized during the device probe phase when
the system boots up. I wonder if the keyboard was absent when your
system was started.

Another note: I think it is NOT recommended to unplug or plug the
keyboard and the PS/2 mouse while the system is running.  The keyboard
and the PS/2 interface is not designed for "hot plugging".  You may
literally FRY your keyboard/mouse and the keyboard controller.  I know
quite a number of people who made this mistake and were left with the
unusable keyboard/mouse and the motherboard in the past...

Kazu
yokota@freebsd.org
